sections.subtitle: Current Form Analysis
sections.text: Mallorca is struggling, positioned 19th with only 1 point and no wins in their last 5 matches, conceding 9 goals. Atlético Madrid, at 13th with 5 points, has shown mixed results with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses in their last 5 matches, but they often score first.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Atlético Madrid dominates recent head-to-head encounters, winning all last 5 matchups against Mallorca, including a 2-0 and a 1-0 victory in the last two games. Mallorca has failed to score in 4 of these matches.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Overview
sections.text: Mallorca has scored only 4 goals this season (15th in league) and struggles with big chances created (18th). Atlético Madrid, while not at their best, shows better attacking intent with early goals (50% in first 15 minutes) and a solid defensive record against weaker teams.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Mallorca's defense is vulnerable, with no clean sheets and frequent first-half deficits. Atlético Madrid’s ability to score early and control games (often first to score in 5/6 recent matches) could exploit Mallorca’s weaknesses.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, the scoreline remains goalless between Mallorca and Atlético Madrid at Estadi de Son Moix. The first half saw a stark contrast in dominance, with Atlético Madrid controlling possession and creating far more opportunities in the attacking third. Mallorca, on the other hand, struggled to maintain the ball and generate meaningful chances, often resorting to a defensive approach. Despite Atlético's pressure, neither team managed to convert their efforts into goals, leaving everything to play for in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Mallorca
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca had a challenging first half, with limited possession and minimal presence in the final third. Their defensive structure held firm, managing to keep Atlético at bay despite the constant pressure. The home side showed resilience in aerial duels and overall duels, narrowly edging out their opponents in these physical battles. However, their lack of attacking intent was evident, as they rarely threatened the Atlético goal. Key defensive contributions came from players like Martin Valjent, who made crucial clearances to thwart danger.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Atlético Madrid
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid dominated much of the first half, showcasing their superiority in possession and territorial control. They created a notable big chance and struck the woodwork, highlighting their ability to penetrate Mallorca's defense. Their frequent entries into the penalty area and possessions won in the final third kept the home side under constant pressure. Despite missing a significant opportunity, players like Julián Álvarez showed promise with dangerous attempts. Atlético will be frustrated not to have taken the lead given their overwhelming control.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half was heavily tilted in favor of Atlético Madrid, who consistently pushed forward with sustained attacking spells throughout the majority of the game. From early on, they established control, with frequent surges into the opposition half that kept Mallorca on the back foot. Mallorca had fleeting moments of forward momentum, particularly at the very start and towards the end of the half, but these were quickly subdued by Atlético's relentless pressure. The away side's attacking rhythm suggests they are far more likely to break the deadlock.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Mallorca, Vedat Muriqi will be crucial in the second half if they are to turn the tide, as his ability to hold up play and create chances could spark a counterattack. On the defensive end, Martin Valjent's composure under pressure will be vital to keep Atlético at bay. For Atlético Madrid, Julián Álvarez stands out as a primary threat after coming close in the first half, and his finishing could prove decisive. Additionally, players like Koke, with their vision and passing, could unlock Mallorca's defense with a well-timed ball.
